strategy_doc_section_loaded: Where is this all going? (M2 — Big Idea, Mood (deliberate), Hero exercise phases, One magic beat is enough, cross-pollination-bonus gating, Arc Logic M2, Mood Arc M2)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named — expanded horizon honored, not flattened
Lead with discipline, not failure mode — no Big Idea section in exercise file
Strategy-fidelity — cross-module sequencing and ordering claims honored
Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts — no Key Concepts section in exercise file
Key Concepts shape — no Key Concepts section in exercise file
What You'll Learn shape — no What You'll Learn section in exercise file
Big Idea match — exercise files carry no Big Idea section; module file owns this
Diverge-first ordering preserved — individual signal → group synthesis → cross-pollination
Cross-pollination gated on group synthesis + Ideas section completion — not unconditional
Phase 3 optional/bonus framing — strategy doc calls it 'Cross-pollination bonus'; file header omits that qualifier
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 3 header reads '## Phase 3. Cross-pollination (20 minutes)' with no bonus/optional label, but body gating language ('You can start Phase 3 as soon as your group synthesis has the Ideas section') and the 'stop when I say stop' instruction preserve the contingent nature. Expanded from ~5 min in strategy doc to 20 min, but the exercise file legitimately unpacks the strategy overview's approximate timing. Gating semantics are preserved; the absent label is cosmetic, not a mood-contract violation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Crux-concept attribution — Rumelt named once, no framework lecture
One magic beat — naming the ordering after students have lived it; reveal in maintainer block not student body
Mood framing scope — expanded-horizon mood is not flattened to graduation ceremony; 'we know what to do next' not 'we're done'
Participant success definition — group synthesis is the success metric; cross-pollination is enrichment
